title: DSDS Theme Definition
description: A theme documents an alternative token-value context such as a color mode, density setting, or brand variant. DSDS captures the purpose, document blocks, and relationships of a theme — the actual override values live in the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) file referenced by the source property. Presentational and classificatory concerns (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links) live in the modular `metadata` array as typed entries. Express internal artifact relationships through metadata link entries rather than a dedicated document block type.

tokenOverride.description: Documents a single token override within a theme — which token is affected, and why. The actual override value lives in the DTCG file; this entry captures the intent and rationale.

token.description: The name of the token being overridden. MUST match the `name` of a token defined elsewhere in the system. Token names are unconstrained by a pattern to support DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary').

description.description: Explains what changes for this token in this theme and why — e.g., 'Lightened to meet WCAG AAA contrast on the dark background.' The resolved value itself is in the DTCG file.

theme.description: Documents a theme — a named context that adapts the design system's token values. DSDS captures the purpose, usage document blocks, accessibility considerations, and the list of affected tokens. The actual override values live in the DTCG file referenced by the source property. Use themes for color modes (light, dark, high-contrast), density settings (compact, comfortable), brand variants, or product-specific customizations. The token catalog remains the authoritative source for token identity, type, and documentation. Presentational and classificatory concerns (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links) live in the modular `metadata` array as typed entries with a `kind` discriminator.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a theme.

displayName.description: Human-readable name for the theme (e.g., 'Dark Mode', 'High Contrast', 'Compact Density').

description.description: A description of the theme — what context it adapts the system to, when to apply it, and how it differs from default token values. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` discriminator. Types include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

source.description: Reference to the authoritative W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this theme's token override definitions.

path.description: The dot-path to this theme's group within the DTCG file, if applicable.

overrides.description: Token overrides that this theme affects. Each entry documents which token is changed and why. Only list tokens whose values differ from the default — unlisted tokens retain their default values. The actual override values live in the DTCG file referenced by source.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this theme as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Themes accept general document block types only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Each document block entry has a `type` property that determines its shape. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.
